source_jobs.title: Senior User Acquisition Specialist
source_jobs.description:
YOUR EXPEDITION: GROWTH & MARKETING AT POLARSTEPS

Over the past 10 years, Polarsteps has grown massively through word-of-mouth, thanks to our product and tech department building a top-quality user experience for all our travelers.

The Growth & Marketing department is a growing team, but already doing big things. You'll take full ownership of our user acquisition strategies, spanning paid flywheels including Meta, TikTok, Google, and Apple Search Ads, to our content flywheels including LLM optimizations, video search, ASO and Reddit engagement to maximize acceleration within our key markets (FR, DE, UK, US, NL). Besides that, you will work closely with our product managers to optimize the user flow for paid users to improve activation rates.

With ratings of 4.8 and 4.7 on the app stores and features in multiple Google and Apple showcases, we've got an impressive past. But we're even more excited for the future, and the impact you can make from day one is huge, directly influencing how we can accelerate user acquisition in all our key markets.




YOUR ITINERARY

What your journey at Polarsteps will look like day-to-day.

You will:

 - Execute, monitor, and optimize performance marketing campaigns, conducting tests, analyzing data, and identifying trends with a focus on lowering CPAs and acquiring high quality users.

 - Collaborate with agencies, UGC creators, Polarsteps Explorers and Travel Creators to develop compelling on-brand creatives that resonate with target audiences.

 - Improve our App Store Rankings for Google Play & iOS Store (ASO) to enhance organic install growth. (We currently work closely with an App Store Optimization (ASO) expert but would like you to start taking it over)

 - Improve our Organic Search strategy on TikTok, YouTube and LLMs to capture users the moment they are planning their next trip.

 - Track, report, and deeply analyze campaign performance, understanding the core problems and drivers of change.




THE EQUIPMENT IN YOUR BACKPACK

What we're hoping you'll bring with you.

✅ 6+ years of experience in performance marketing (e.g., Apple Search Ads, Google App campaigns, Meta and TikTok) at mobile app companies.

✅ A solid understanding of the mobile tracking and measurement ecosystem (e.g. SKAD, AEM, ATT, etc.).

✅ Hands-on experience with product analytics and reporting tools (e.g., Mixpanel, Amplitude, Appsflyer, Adjust, Looker, Metabase etc.).

✅ A data-driven mindset with the ability to turn complex information into clear, actionable insights.

✅ Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to present analyses and findings in clear, impactful formats.




1ST CLASS POLARSTEPS EXTRAS

Some cool things to make the journey extra sweet.

 - A yearly free flight or train ride to a random location! Give our Teleporter a spin here. https://www.polarsteps.com/teleporter

 - A shiny MacBook to work your magic on.

 - Hybrid working, our way. Work from home and the office, we ask everyone to be in the office 2 days a week. It's how we stay flexible, focused, and meaningfully connected in person.

 - An extensive home office set-up, everything from noise-canceling headphones to a standing desk.

 - 30 days of vacation (for adventures or sitting on the couch, it's up to you).

 - Two paid Culture Days for days that are important to you but not recognized by the official Dutch calendar.

 - The option to switch up your zoom background by working from another country for up to 8 weeks a year.

 - A personal development budget for that curious mind of yours!

 - A pension plan to help you save for those around-the-world retirement trips.

 - Delicious daily team lunches.

 - Awesome monthly parties, as well as team trips away.

 - A lively office in downtown Amsterdam, with travel costs reimbursed.

 - Free Polarsteps Travel Books to contain all your far-flung adventures.



At Polarsteps, we believe the best products are built by people as diverse as the travelers using

source_jobs.title: iOS Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Looking for a new adventure? You’ve come to the right place.

We're an Amsterdam-based team of 90+ (and counting) with 25+ nationalities amongst us! A varied bunch of “Citizens of The World” with a wide range of skills and hundreds of thousands of kilometers under our travel belts. And we have something important in common (other than our very well-worn passports): We want to change the way people travel.

And that's exactly what we're doing, with the travel app to plan, track, and relive trips in a smart and beautiful way.

Polarsteps has over 20 million users already, but, for us, this is just the beginning. We have big plans for the future, and so we need even more talented and enthusiastic people to help us on our journey.

We want to change the way people travel.

And that's exactly what we're doing, with the travel app to plan, track, and relive trips in a smart and beautiful way.

Polarsteps has over 20 million users already but, for us, this is just the beginning. We have exciting new features on our roadmap and we need a talented iOS Engineer to help us on our journey.

Want to know more about us? Here https://careers.polarsteps.com/ you go.




YOUR EXPEDITION: IOS DEVELOPMENT AT POLARSTEPS

We’re a small team, doing big things.

If you haven't seen our app yet, go ahead and check it out, you'll see that we care (a lot) about providing a seamless, top-quality user experience for every one of our travellers. And it is this that has got us ratings of 4.8* and 4.7* on the app stores (where we’ve been featured multiple times). But it doesn’t stop there, not only did Google create this https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nYcBqtOwLcg awesome video about us but we’ve been mentioned in two of Apple’s WWDC keynote speeches. And we're one of the top 26 apps for 2026 https://apps.apple.com/ie/iphone/story/id1849362474 according to Apple!

We’ve got an impressive past but we’re even more excited for the future, and the impact you can make from day one is huge, with everything you ship affecting millions of users.

Even with that in mind, we believe in truly giving our team members the freedom they need to craft to perfection while encouraging a practical mindset to avoid under or over-engineering mishaps.

We are seeing an enthusiastic and accelerated growth of the travel industry, and with that comes both opportunities and growing pains (especially in the complex, multi-layered travel domain). So, if on top of the challenges inherent to feature development, you enjoy thinking about the performance and scalability aspects of a platform, you're in for a lot of fun with us.




YOUR ITINERARY

What your journey at Polarsteps will look like day-to-day.

You will:

 - Working closely with Jimmy (our Lead iOS Engineer), and Javi, Andrii, Orcun, Arne, Lieneke, Daniil, Rosa, Dmytro, Azat and Paulo (our senior iOS developers) on all things related to our app.

 - Be responsible for implementing new features, improving existing features and maintaining the day-to-day operations of our iOS App.

 - We're product-first; meaning you won't just work within the iOS team, you'll be embedded in a dedicated Product Pillar team, each owning a distinct part of the user journey. Day-to-day you'll work closely with PMs, designers, and engineers across disciplines. It's a genuinely cross-functional setup: PMs, designers, and engineers working together from problem to solution, because the best product decisions happen when technical perspective is in the room early.

 - Proactively improving the maintainability, resiliency and extensibility of our app, addressing technical debt when needed or even better, preventing it when it makes sense.




THE EQUIPMENT IN YOUR BACKPACK

What we’re hoping you’ll bring with you.

 - At least 3-5 years of hands-on experience with iOS development

 - Experience working in an agile team environment.

 - A pragmatic approach to problem-solving, and an absence of fear when it comes to tackling big challenges in UI, archite

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Looking for a new adventure? You’ve come to the right place.



We're an Amsterdam-based team of 90+ (and counting) with 25+ nationalities amongst us! A varied bunch of “Citizens of The World” with a wide range of skills and hundreds of thousands of kilometers under our travel belts. And we have something important in common (other than our very well-worn passports): We want to change the way people travel. 

And that's exactly what we're doing, with the travel app to plan, track, and relive trips in a smart and beautiful way.

Polarsteps has over 20 million users already, but, for us, this is just the beginning. We have big plans for the future, and so we need even more talented and enthusiastic people to help us on our journey.

Want to know more about us? Here https://careers.polarsteps.com/ you go.




YOUR EXPEDITION: PRODUCT DESIGN AT POLARSTEPS

We’re a small team, doing big things.

At Polarsteps, everyone, our CEO, support specialists, developers, product managers, and more, cares about design. It’s part of our DNA and embedded in every discussion. This gives you, as a Product Designer, the opportunity to make full use of your skills and craft your designs to perfection.

Our relatively small team consists of like-minded design enthusiasts who can geek out over the nitty-gritty details–corner radius, shadows, colors–for hours. We are big on collaboration, challenge each other, and make meetings as fun as they are productive. We dream big, move fast and welcome ideas, from anyone.




YOUR ITINERARY

What your journey at Polarsteps will look like on a typical day.

 - Lead the design of our "Plan" team at Polarsteps, focused on one of travel's most anticipation-filled moments: charting the path ahead. We're building intuitive, inspiring new ways for travelers to plot their journeys and bring their dream itineraries to life.

 - Oversee the full product lifecycle of concepting, prototyping, and iterating based on design reviews, user testing, and feedback.

 - Implement and maintain the existing design & brand guidelines while also creating and adapting guidelines to elevate the overall Polarsteps design.

 - Own your product pillar, while maintaining close collaboration with the other pillars. Proactively ask for and share feedback to keep our product smooth, consistent, and awesome.

 - Collaborate with Product Managers and Engineers to ensure your designs are well-specced and built as intended, down to the last pixel and color.




THE EQUIPMENT IN YOUR BACKPACK

What we’re hoping you’ll bring with you.

✅ Being a seasoned professional with 5+ years of experience designing lovable products;

✅ A strong portfolio, with shipped designs, that demonstrates end-to-end (hybrid UX and UI) product design;

✅ Experience designing for native mobile apps;

✅ Hands-on experience with user insights and testing, like A/B tests and usability studies;

✅ A proactive mindset, with a passion for getting involved in the entire product development process, thinking and problem-solving like a product manager;

✅ Fluency in modern design tools such as Figma/Sketch or similar;

✅ Experience in prototyping tools such as Principle/Protopie or similar;

✅ Skills in partnering with engineers to drive QA processes and uphold implementation fidelity.




1ST CLASS EXTRAS

Some cool things to make the journey extra sweet.

 - A yearly free flight or train ride to a random location! Give our Teleporter a spin here. https://www.polarsteps.com/teleporter

 - A shiny new MacBook to work your magic on.

 - Hybrid working, our way. Work from home and the office, we ask everyone to be in the office 2 days a week. It’s how we stay flexible, focused, and meaningfully connected in person.

 - An extensive home office set-up, everything from noise-canceling headphones to a standing desk.

 - 30 days of vacation (for adventures or sitting on the couch, it's up to you)

 - Two paid Culture Days for days that are important to you but not recognized by the offi

Even with that in mind, we believe in truly giving our team members the freedom they need to craft to perfection while encouraging a practical mindset to avoid under- or over-engineering mishaps.

We are seeing enthusiastic and accelerated growth of the travel industry, and with that comes both opportunities and growing pains (especially in the complex, multi-layered travel domain). YOUR ITINERARY

What your journey at Polarsteps will look like day-to-day.

You will:

 - Act as a true people manager for your team - owning career development, performance conversations, and creating an environment where engineers grow, feel supported, and do their best work.

 - Be accountable for team health and performance: delivery, quality, incident response, knowledge sharing, and sustainable on-call practices.

 - On top of your people leadership responsibilities, carry out duties as an individual contributor such as:
   
   - Collaborating with Product Development teams, PMs, designers, and other engineers to enhance Polarsteps' features and functionalities.
   
   - Leading technical projects that improve scalability, reliability, and performance.
   
   - Developing and maintaining our main API and related services, ensuring seamless support for web and mobile applications used by travelers globally.
   
   - Maintaining an efficient development process, including timely code reviews, streamlined meetings, and effective asynchronous communication.
   
   - Partnering with stakeholders to prioritize tasks, align with quarterly and yearly OKRs and enhance the overall developer experience.

 - Scale and evolve our infrastructure to handle continued growth (we have millions of active users), with reliability, performance, and observability top of mind.

 - Raise the technical bar through hands-on code reviews, architectural guidance, and clear standards.

 - Be AI-pilled, but responsibly. You are on top of and experiment with the fast-changing landscape of AI tooling, and act as an evangelist within the team - but always understanding its current limitations and ensuring its usage is properly de-risked.




THE EQUIPMENT IN YOUR BACKPACK

What we’re hoping you’ll bring with you.

 - Proven experience leading and growing backend en

 - Maintain an efficient development process, including timely code reviews, streamlined meetings, and effective asynchronous communication.

 - Partner with stakeholders to prioritize tasks, align with quarterly and yearly OKRs and enhance the overall developer experience with the guidance of the Tech Lead and fellow backend engineers.

✅ 6+ years of experience developing complex backend systems for web platforms, proficiency in Python

✅ Strong background in relational databases (we use PostgreSQL) and messaging queues (e.g., Celery, RabbitMQ)

✅ You build leverage through AI-assisted engineering, and at the right level: not blind trust, but confident delegation for accelerating your output.

✅ Experience using one of the cloud service providers (AWS, Google, Azure)

✅ Have built and scaled applications processing thousands of requests per second

✅ Solid understanding of observability and monitoring practices

✅ Solid understanding of the web stack

✅ Pragmatic problem-solving approach, balancing quick solutions with structural changes when necessary.

✅ Excellent English communication skills, both written and spoken.



Bonus points for:

 - Proficiency in FastAPI.

 - Having worked in a consumer-facing product.

 - Being product-minded.

Some cool things to make the journey extra sweet.

source_jobs.title: Data Scientist Experimentation
source_jobs.description:
YOUR EXPEDITION AT POLARSTEPS - DATA SCIENCE

Over the past 10 years, Polarsteps has grown massively through word-of-mouth, thanks to our product and tech department building a top-quality user experience for all our travelers.

As our data scientist with expertise in experimentation, you will sit within the Growth & Marketing department of the organization. You will be partnering with the Head of Growth, and will help drive the experimentation practices and analyses, collaborating with product, marketing, designers and engineers, to enable trustworthy decisions at scale. You will help Polarsteps use novel ways (sprt, stratified random sampling, etc.,) to increase the speed of the experimentation lifecycle and develop ways to ensure that our experiment process is the highest standard possible.

With 20M+ users and ratings of 4.8 and 4.7 on the app stores and features in multiple Google and Apple showcases, we've got an impressive past. But we’re even more excited for the future, and you’ll play a crucial role in the growth and direction of Polarsteps as we grow to 100M+ users around the globe.




YOUR ITINERARY

What your journey at Polarsteps will look like day-to-day.

 - Partner with product, growth, and marketing teams to translate business questions into well-designed experiments with clear success criteria.

 - Design, run, and analyze experiments across product and marketing to help Polarsteps make confident, data-backed decisions at scale.

 - Advance our experimentation methodology (introducing techniques like SPRT, stratified sampling, and variance reduction) to help us learn faster and ship with more confidence.

 - Build and maintain the statistical frameworks and tooling that power our experimentation practice, from sample size calculators to automated analysis pipelines.

 - Present experiment results and strategic insights to senior stakeholders, turning statistical findings into clear, actionable recommendations.




THE EQUIPMENT IN YOUR BACKPACK

What we’re hoping you’ll bring with you.

✅ 5+ years of related industry experience in the data science and experimentation domain

✅ Worked with B2C native mobile apps

✅ Expertise in SQL, familiarity with Python, knowledge cloud data environments (e.g. AWS, Clickhouse etc.)

✅ Hands-on experience building and scaling experimentation practices, working with product managers or marketing department and have worked with statistical methods (causal inference, multi-arm bandits. reinforcement learning, synthetic data and experimentation, non-parametric methods, etc.,) in start-ups or scale-ups

✅ Are familiar with product analytics and reporting tools (e.g., Mixpanel, Amplitude, Appsflyer, Adjust, Looker, Meta Base etc.).

✅ Ability to communicate and explain data science and experimentation concepts to diverse audiences and craft a compelling story.

✅ Intellectually curious, creative, and diligent you enjoy thinking about the business as much as about the data.

✅ Focus on business practicality and the 80/20 rule; very high bar for output quality, but recognize the business benefit of "having something now" vs "perfection sometime in the future"




1ST CLASS POLARSTEPS EXTRAS

Some cool things to make the journey extra sweet.

 - A yearly free flight or train ride to a random location! Give our Teleporter a spin here. https://www.polarsteps.com/teleporter

 - A shiny MacBook to work your magic on.

 - Hybrid working, our way. Work from home and the office, we ask everyone to be in the office 2 days a week. It’s how we stay flexible, focused, and meaningfully connected in person.

 - An extensive home office set-up, everything from noise-canceling headphones to a standing desk.

 - 30 days of vacation (for adventures or sitting on the couch, it's up to you).

 - Two paid Culture Days for days that are important to you but not recognized by the official Dutch calendar.

 - The option to switch up your zoom background by working from another country for up to 8 weeks a

source_jobs.title: Data Scientist Growth Analytics
source_jobs.description:
YOUR EXPEDITION AT POLARSTEPS - DATA SCIENCE

Over the past 10 years, Polarsteps has grown massively through word-of-mouth, thanks to our product and tech department building a top-quality user experience for all our travelers.

As our data scientist you will sit within the Growth & Marketing department of the organization. You will help shape Polarsteps growth strategy by analyzing our constraints, developing models, and measuring the impact of product, growth and business initiatives. Partnering with the Head of Growth, you’ll turn data into strategies that guide prioritization of new initiatives and accelerate insights for our product teams. You’ll collaborate with our cross-functional teams of product, marketing and designers to scale insights and drive efficient growth.

With 20M+ users and ratings of 4.8 and 4.7 on the app stores and features in multiple Google and Apple showcases, we've got an impressive past. But we’re even more excited for the future, and you’ll play a crucial role in the growth and direction of Polarsteps as we grow to 100M+ users around the globe.




YOUR ITINERARY

What your journey at Polarsteps will look like day-to-day.

 - Develop data-driven strategies to drive the growth of Polarsteps users in our key marktes

 - Apply statistical methods to deep-dive and breaking down changes to find/understand causal relationships to help inform business decisions.

 - Build, monitor and sustain scalable dashboards to track our growth, engagement and monetization metrics.

 - Communicate to the organization as a whole about growth metrics to help them understand what’s working and what isn’t and help them understand how to drive up MAUs

 - Contribute to quarterly and yearly planning through growth modellingen and impact forecasting.

 - Collaborate closely with our product managers, growth and marketing department to understand growth drivers and guide strategic decisions.

 - Present insights and recommendations to senior stakeholders, influencing the course of our business

 - Support our senior management team with reporting to external parties (current and prospective investors)




THE EQUIPMENT IN YOUR BACKPACK

What we’re hoping you’ll bring with you.

✅ 5+ years analyzing insights from data using tools such as Python/R and SQL.

✅ Hands-on experience supporting B2C Mobile app organizations with their growth strategy

✅ Intellectually curious, creative, and diligent - you enjoy thinking about the business as much as about the data

✅ Worked with product analytics and reporting tools (e.g., Mixpanel, Amplitude, Appsflyer, Adjust, Looker, Meta Base etc.).

✅ Have a background in Economics/Business, Computer Science, Statistics, Engineering or other relevant field.

✅ Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with the ability to present analyses and insights in clear, impactful formats.




1ST CLASS POLARSTEPS EXTRAS

Some cool things to make the journey extra sweet.

 - A yearly free flight or train ride to a random location! Give our Teleporter a spin here. https://www.polarsteps.com/teleporter

 - A shiny MacBook to work your magic on.

 - Hybrid working, our way. Work from home and the office, we ask everyone to be in the office 2 days a week. It’s how we stay flexible, focused, and meaningfully connected in person.

 - An extensive home office set-up, everything from noise-canceling headphones to a standing desk.

 - 30 days of vacation (for adventures or sitting on the couch, it's up to you).

 - Two paid Culture Days for days that are important to you but not recognized by the official Dutch calendar.

 - The option to switch up your zoom background by working from another country for up to 8 weeks a year.

 - A personal development budget for that curious mind of yours!

 - A pension plan to help you save for those around-the-world retirement trips.

 - Delicious daily team lunches.

 - Awesome monthly parties, as well as team trips away.

 - A lively office in downtown Amsterd

source_jobs.title: Junior Creative - Performance Marketing (student)
source_jobs.description:
Looking for a new adventure? You’ve come to the right place. We're an Amsterdam-based team of 90+ (and counting) with 25+ nationalities amongst us! A varied bunch of Citizens of The World with a wide range of skills and hundreds of thousands of kilometers under our travel belts. And we have something important in common (other than our very well-worn passports): we want to change the way people travel.



And that's exactly what we're doing, with the travel app to plan, track, and relive trips in a smart and beautiful way. Polarsteps has over 20 million users already, but, for us, this is just the beginning. We have big plans for the future, and so we need even more talented and enthusiastic people to help us on our journey.



We’re looking for students or graduates who want to join us for a minimum of 24 hours each week. This is a chance to see close-up what life in a startup/scaleup is like, and to join a team building one of the world’s most ambitious consumer brands. This will provide valuable experience and insight for anyone who wants to go into tech/startup life. In 10 years, we think you’ll be proud to say you worked at Polarsteps back when it was just taking off and was under 100 people.




YOUR EXPEDITION AT POLARSTEPS - CREATIVE & PERFORMANCE MARKETING

Over the past 10 years, Polarsteps has grown massively through word-of-mouth, thanks to our product and tech department building a top-quality user experience for all our travelers. The last 2 years, we also started with building a paid layer on top of that already really strong word-of-mouth flywheel to accelerate our growth internationally.

As our creative - performance marketing, you will be working closely with our Marketing Lead, User Acquisition Specialist and Creator Marketer. Your main responsibility is creating compelling visual content that helps drive performance marketing campaigns across our core paid media channels.




YOUR ITINERARY

What your journey at Polarsteps will look like day-to-day.

 - Video editing content creation - filming and editing videos to produce full funnel, thumb-stopping performance marketing assets.

 - UGC collaboration - Editing our ambassadors or other UGC’s content to create next level assets that can be used across our performance marketing.

 - Photography - shooting and editing to build up our library of great content for use in ads and Polarsteps communications.

 - Graphic design - designing graphics for our performance marketing campaigns whether that’s Instagram ads, TikTok ads, Google ads or a new channel completely.

 - Campaign ideation - helping to come up with ideas and performance marketing campaigns across the year.

 - Supporting the user acquisition specialist in reviewing creative assets, ensuring that every piece of content proudly represents the Polarsteps brand and that consistent branding is maintained across all creative outputs.

Please note: this role does not include the management or production of content for organic social media channels.




THE EQUIPMENT IN YOUR BACKPACK

What we’re hoping you’ll bring with you.

 - Ability to work minimum of 24 hours per week alongside your studies or summer break.

 - Fluency in English or native fluency in French and/or German

 - Bringing visual ideas to life: You have a passion for creating stunning visuals, whether through photography, graphic design, or video.

 - Designing for Impact: You understand the power of design in driving performance. You’re skilled at creating graphics for social media and ad campaigns that not only look great but also convert. You can design with different platforms in mind, ensuring your work is optimized for Instagram, TikTok, Google ads, and beyond.

 - You know how to capture the essence of a brand in every piece of content you create, and you thrive on producing eye-catching visuals that resonate with audiences.
